headline: AbbVie Stock's Growth Story Has One Question Mark
summary: The company's post-Humira plan is working brilliantly, but a subtle shift in the growth rate of one of its two key drugs deserves a much closer look.

summary: Major M&A deals this week include: Adobe buys Topaz Labs, Rise Baking Company purchases Jimmy's Gourmet Bakery.

headline: AbbVie to Host Second-Quarter 2026 Earnings Conference Call
summary: AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV) will announce its second-quarter 2026 financial results on Friday, July 31, 2026, before the market opens. AbbVie will host a live webcast of the earnings conference call at 8 a.m. Central time. It will be accessible through AbbVie's Investor Relations website at investors.abbvie.com. An archived edition of the session will be available later that day.

headline: The Homebody Portfolio: How Much Does Retirement Cost When You Never Leave Chicago?
summary: What does retirement look like for someone who genuinely has no desire to leave home? No snowbird condo in Florida. No Viking river cruises. No RV parked outside Yellowstone. Just Chicago. The Cubs in the summer, the museums when the weather turns, dinner at the neighborhood Italian restaurant, church on Sunday, and grandchildren across town ... The Homebody Portfolio: How Much Does Retirement Cost When You Never Leave Chicago?

headline: Jazz Pharmaceuticals: Why It's Time To Cash Out (Rating Downgrade)
summary: Jazz Pharmaceuticals' shares are in the distribution phase because Zepzelca failed to show a survival benefit over irinotecan. See why JAZZ stock is downgraded to hold.

headline: Will AbbVie's (ABBV) Pediatric Expansions Reveal a Deeper Shift in Its Growth Mix?
summary: Earlier this week, AbbVie reported European Commission approvals extending Skyrizi to children with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis and making Maviret the only therapy cleared in the EU for both acute and chronic hepatitis C infection from age three. These label expansions broaden AbbVie’s reach in immunology and virology by opening earlier treatment windows and adding pediatric populations in high-need diseases. We’ll now explore how Skyrizi’s new pediatric indication and Maviret’s...
